slogan_jsonb.zh: Anthropic 黑客马拉松冠军打造的 Claude Code 终极增强套件
slogan_jsonb.en: The Ultimate Agent Harness & Performance Optimization System for Claude Code

description_jsonb.zh: Everything Claude Code 是由 Anthropic 黑客马拉松冠军 Affaan Malik 开源的 [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) 全栈增强工具集，GitHub 累计获得 50,000+ Stars。该项目将 9 个生产级智能体、11 个可复用技能、11 条自定义命令、10 个生命周期钩子、跨会话记忆持久化机制以及一套完整的安全扫描红队框架整合为一体，通过一行命令即可完成安装。它同时兼容 [Cursor](https://cursor.sh)、[Codex](https://openai.com/index/openai-codex/)、[Opencode](https://opencode.ai) 等主流 AI 编码工具，形成一套以
为核心理念的 AI 驱动软件工程范式，将上下文感知能力和 Token 效率优化提升至生产环境所需水准。
description_jsonb.en: Everything Claude Code is a full-stack enhancement toolkit for [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code) open-sourced by Anthropic hackathon winner Affaan Malik, with 50,000+ GitHub Stars. It consolidates 9 production-grade agents, 11 reusable skills, 11 custom commands, 10 lifecycle hooks, cross-session memory persistence, and a complete security red-teaming framework into a single one-line install. Compatible with [Cursor](https://cursor.sh), [Codex](https://openai.com/index/openai-codex/), and [Opencode](https://opencode.ai), it establishes a research-first, security-first AI-driven software engineering paradigm that elevates context awareness and token efficiency to production-grade standards.

painPoints: 原生 Claude Code 缺乏跨会话记忆：每次新对话 Claude 都从零开始，无法记住项目架构、编码规范或历史决策，导致大型项目中反复解释同一上下文，严重浪费 Token 预算
无内置安全防护机制：裸跑 Claude Code 时，AI 可能在未审查的情况下执行危险操作（如删文件、覆盖配置），缺少权限管控和行为审计日志
生命周期钩子缺失：无法在代码生成前后自动触发代码检查、测试运行、文档更新等工程流程，需要开发者手动介入，破坏 AI 驱动的自动化闭环
单智能体局限：原生配置下无法协调多个专业化子智能体并行处理不同模块，复杂项目中任务调度完全依赖单次对话上下文，效率瓶颈明显
innovations: 持久化记忆架构：通过结构化的 CLAUDE.md 规范和 .claude/memory 目录实现项目级长期记忆注入，让 Claude 在每次会话开始时即掌握完整项目语境，彻底解决「AI 遗忘症」
多智能体协作框架：内置 9 个专业化智能体（研究员、架构师、安全审计员等），通过 Subagent 调度协议实现并行任务分解，单次任务可拆解为多个独立工作流并发执行
安全红队扫描引擎：内置安全扫描技能可对 Claude Code 的配置、权限和代码产出进行主动红队测试，识别提示注入、权限滥用等风险，是业内少有的将安全内置于 AI 编码工具的开源方案
跨工具生态兼容：同一套配置文件可无缝适配 Claude Code、Cursor、Codex、Opencode 等多个主流 AI 编码环境，开发者无需为不同工具维护多套最佳实践规范
Token 效率优化系统：通过技能模块化、上下文压缩和分层记忆注入机制，在等量 Token 消耗下，使 AI 产出的代码质量和任务完成率显著提升

painPoints: No cross-session memory in native Claude Code: every new conversation starts from scratch, forcing developers to re-explain project architecture and coding conventions repeatedly, wasting significant token budgets on large codebases
Zero built-in security guardrails: unenhanced Claude Code may execute destructive operations (file deletion, config overwrite) without review, lacking permission controls or behavioral audit logs

Missing lifecycle hooks: no mechanism to automatically trigger linting, test runs, or documentation updates pre/post code generation, breaking the AI-driven automation loop and requiring manual developer intervention

Single-agent bottleneck: native configuration cannot coordinate multiple specialized sub-agents in parallel, making complex project task scheduling entirely dependent on single-conversation context with clear efficiency ceilings
innovations: Persistent memory architecture: structured CLAUDE.md specs and .claude/memory directories enable project-level long-term memory injection, so Claude enters every session with full project context, eliminating 'AI amnesia'

Multi-agent orchestration framework: 9 built-in specialized agents (researcher, architect, security auditor, etc.) leverage a subagent scheduling protocol for parallel task decomposition, allowing a single complex task to be split into concurrent independent workflows

Security red-team scanning engine: built-in security scanning skill actively red-teams Claude Code configurations, permissions, and code output to identify prompt injection and privilege abuse risks — one of the few open-source AI coding tools with security natively built in

Cross-tool ecosystem compatibility: a single configuration set works seamlessly across Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, and Opencode, eliminating the need to maintain separate best-practice documentation for different AI coding environments

Token efficiency optimization: through skill modularization, context compression, and layered memory injection, code quality and task completion rates improve significantly for equivalent token consumption

zh.title: 智能体层 (Agents Layer)
zh.desc: 项目核心是位于 /agents 目录下的 9 个生产级专业化智能体，涵盖研究员（Research Agent）、架构师（Architect Agent）、安全审计员（Security Agent）等角色。每个智能体拥有独立的系统提示（System Prompt）、工具调用权限白名单和上下文窗口管理策略，通过 AGENTS.md 中定义的 Subagent 调度协议协同工作。这种分工设计使得复杂任务可以被分解为多个专业化子任务并行处理，避免了单一通才智能体在大型工程任务中因上下文膨胀导致的质量下降，是整个系统吞吐量和代码质量的根本保障。

zh.title: 技能模块系统 (Skills Module)
zh.desc: 位于 /skills 目录的 11 个可复用技能模块是该系统的知识封装单元，每个技能是一个结构化的 Markdown 文件，包含特定领域的专家知识、代码模式和最佳实践。当 Claude 执行任务时，相关技能会被按需注入到上下文中，实现精准的领域知识增强。这种设计与传统 RAG（检索增强生成）的核心思想一致，但针对代码生成场景做了深度优化：技能内容是高密度的结构化知识，而非原始文档，使得每个 Token 的信息密度最大化，在不超出上下文窗口限制的前提下注入最多的有效知识。

zh.title: 生命周期钩子引擎 (Lifecycle Hooks)
zh.desc: 位于 /hooks 目录的 10 个生命周期钩子是连接 Claude 与外部工程工具链的桥梁，在 Pre-Tool（工具调用前）、Post-Tool（工具调用后）、Pre-Edit（文件修改前）、Post-Edit（文件修改后）等关键节点自动触发。例如，Pre-Edit 钩子可在 Claude 修改任何文件前强制运行代码静态分析，Post-Edit 钩子可自动触发单元测试和文档同步更新。这套钩子机制将 AI 编码从「一次性代码生成」升级为「闭环质量保障流程」，是整个系统中实现真正工程化自动化的关键设施。

zh.title: MCP 配置与工具集成 (MCP Config Layer)
zh.desc: 位于 /mcp-configs 目录的 Model Context Protocol 配置层定义了 Claude 可调用的外部工具集，包括文件系统、浏览器自动化、数据库查询、API 调用等能力的精细化权限配置。MCP 是 Anthropic 推出的开放标准，用于规范 AI 模型与外部工具的交互接口。本项目通过预置多套经过安全审计的 MCP 配置模板，让开发者能够在享受强大工具调用能力的同时，将权限边界限制在最小必要范围内，有效防止 AI 越权操作带来的安全风险。

zh.title: 跨会话记忆持久化 (Memory Persistence)
zh.desc: 系统通过两层记忆机制实现跨会话知识持久化：第一层是位于项目根目录的 CLAUDE.md 文件，存储项目级的架构决策、编码规范和技术债务记录，每次 Claude Code 启动时自动读取；第二层是 .claude/memory 目录下的动态记忆文件，在会话过程中由特定的 Memory Hook 自动捕获并结构化存储 Claude 的关键决策和发现。双层架构将「静态项目文档」与「动态 AI 认知」分离管理，在保持记忆持久性的同时，避免了无限膨胀的记忆文件拖慢上下文加载速度。

en.title: Agents Layer
en.desc: The system core is 9 production-grade specialized agents housed in the /agents directory, covering roles including Research Agent, Architect Agent, and Security Agent. Each agent has its own system prompt, tool-call permission whitelist, and context window management strategy, collaborating via the subagent scheduling protocol defined in AGENTS.md. This division of labor allows complex tasks to be decomposed into specialized parallel sub-tasks, preventing the quality degradation that occurs when a single generalist agent handles large engineering tasks with bloated context — this is the fundamental guarantee of the system's throughput and code quality.

en.title: Skills Module System
en.desc: The 11 reusable skill modules in the /skills directory serve as the system's knowledge encapsulation units. Each skill is a structured Markdown file containing domain expert knowledge, code patterns, and best practices. When Claude executes tasks, relevant skills are injected into context on demand for precision domain augmentation. This design aligns with core RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) principles but is deeply optimized for code generation: skill content is high-density structured knowledge rather than raw documents, maximizing information density per token and injecting maximum effective knowledge without exceeding context window limits.

en.title: Lifecycle Hooks Engine
en.desc: The 10 lifecycle hooks in the /hooks directory bridge Claude with external engineering toolchains, triggering automatically at critical nodes including Pre-Tool, Post-Tool, Pre-Edit, and Post-Edit. For example, a Pre-Edit hook can enforce static code analysis before Claude modifies any file, while a Post-Edit hook can auto-trigger unit tests and documentation sync. This hook mechanism upgrades AI coding from 'one-shot code generation' to a 'closed-loop quality assurance process,' making it the key infrastructure for achieving true engineering automation within the system.

en.title: MCP Config & Tool Integration
en.desc: The Model Context Protocol configuration layer in /mcp-configs defines the external toolset Claude can invoke, including fine-grained permission configurations for filesystem access, browser automation, database queries, and API calls. MCP is an open standard released by Anthropic to normalize the interaction interface between AI models and external tools. By pre-bundling multiple security-audited MCP configuration templates, this project lets developers enjoy powerful tool-calling capabilities while restricting permission boundaries to the minimum necessary scope, effectively preventing security risks from AI privilege escalation.

en.title: Cross-Session Memory Persistence
en.desc: The system achieves cross-session knowledge persistence through a two-layer memory mechanism. The first layer is the CLAUDE.md file in the project root, storing project-level architectural decisions, coding conventions, and technical debt records, auto-loaded each time Claude Code starts. The second layer is dynamic memory files under .claude/memory, automatically captured and structured by Memory Hooks during sessions to preserve Claude's key decisions and discoveries. This dual-layer architecture separates 'static project documentation' from 'dynamic AI cognition,' maintaining memory persistence while preventing infinitely expanding memory files from slowing context loading.

zh.instruction: 一行命令安装（推荐）：通过 Claude Code 插件市场直接安装，无需任何额外配置
或者通过 npm 全局安装（适用于需要自定义的场景）
手动克隆仓库并运行安装脚本（完整自定义控制）
zh.code:
git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code.git
cd everything-claude-code
bash install.sh

zh.instruction: 验证安装：在 Claude Code 中运行以下命令查看已加载的智能体和技能列表

en.instruction: One-line install (recommended): Install directly via Claude Code plugin marketplace with no additional configuration required
Or install globally via npm (for scenarios requiring customization)
Manually clone the repository and run the install script (full custom control)
en.code:
git clone https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code.git
cd everything-claude-code
bash install.sh

en.instruction: Verify installation: run the following commands in Claude Code to view loaded agents and skills

zh: 大型代码库重构加速: Who: 中大型团队的后端工程师; What: 利用多智能体并行分析架构、生成重构方案、执行代码迁移，配合生命周期钩子自动运行测试和 lint; Outcome: 将数周的人工重构压缩至数天，且过程中代码质量持续受到自动化闭环保障
安全合规代码审计: Who: 需要通过 SOC2/ISO27001 审计的企业安全工程师; What: 利用内置安全红队技能对代码库进行提示注入检测、权限配置审查和 OWASP Top10 模式扫描; Outcome: 在人工安全审查前自动识别 80% 以上的常见安全漏洞，大幅降低合规审计成本
AI 原生新产品快速原型: Who: 独立开发者或初创公司 CTO; What: 通过持久化记忆和专业化智能体在多个开发会话间保持完整的架构决策上下文，加速从 0 到 MVP 的全栈开发; Outcome: 实现跨会话的连续性开发体验，上下文维护成本降为零，MVP 交付周期可缩短 40%-60%
en: Large-Scale Codebase Refactoring: Who: Backend engineers at mid-to-large teams; What: Use multi-agent parallel analysis to generate refactoring plans and execute code migrations, with lifecycle hooks auto-running tests and lint throughout; Outcome: Compress weeks of manual refactoring into days with continuous automated quality assurance throughout the process

Security Compliance Code Auditing: Who: Enterprise security engineers undergoing SOC2/ISO27001 audits; What: Use built-in security red-team skills to perform prompt injection detection, permission config reviews, and OWASP Top10 pattern scanning across codebases; Outcome: Automatically identify 80%+ of common security vulnerabilities before manual security review, significantly reducing compliance audit costs

AI-Native Product Rapid Prototyping: Who: Indie developers or startup CTOs; What: Leverage persistent memory and specialized agents to maintain complete architectural decision context across multiple development sessions, accelerating full-stack development from 0 to MVP; Outcome: Achieve continuous cross-session development with zero context maintenance overhead, reducing MVP delivery cycles by 40%-60%

zh: 强绑定 Claude Code 生态：核心功能深度依赖 Anthropic 的 Claude Code CLI 和 MCP 协议，虽然声称兼容 Cursor/Codex，但部分高级钩子和智能体特性在非 Claude Code 环境下功能受限
Token 消耗不透明：多智能体并发和技能注入会显著增加每次任务的 Token 消耗量，在 Claude API 按量计费模式下，复杂任务的实际成本可能超出预期，且当前缺乏精细化的 Token 预算控制面板
学习曲线陡峭：对于不熟悉 Claude Code 底层机制（如 MCP、Subagent 协议、钩子生命周期）的开发者，充分发挥所有 9 个智能体和 11 个技能的协同威力需要相当的前置学习投入
社区贡献质量参差不齐：作为快速增长的开源项目，部分社区贡献的技能和智能体模板缺乏充分测试，可能在特定项目类型或语言栈下产生不稳定输出
Windows 支持不完整：install.sh 脚本和部分钩子依赖 Unix 环境，Windows 用户需通过 WSL2 运行，原生 PowerShell 环境下存在已知兼容性问题
en: Strong Claude Code ecosystem lock-in: core features deeply depend on Anthropic's Claude Code CLI and MCP protocol; while Cursor/Codex compatibility is claimed, advanced hooks and agent features are functionally limited in non-Claude Code environments

Opaque token consumption: multi-agent concurrency and skill injection significantly increase per-task token usage; in Claude API pay-as-you-go billing mode, actual costs for complex tasks may exceed expectations, and the project currently lacks a fine-grained token budget control panel

Steep learning curve: developers unfamiliar with Claude Code's underlying mechanics (MCP, subagent protocols, hook lifecycles) face substantial upfront learning investment to fully leverage the synergy of all 9 agents and 11 skills

Inconsistent community contribution quality: as a rapidly growing open-source project, some community-contributed skill and agent templates lack thorough testing and may produce unstable outputs for specific project types or language stacks
Incomplete Windows support: the install.sh script and several hooks depend on Unix environments; Windows users must run via WSL2, with known compatibility issues in native PowerShell

zh.question: Everything Claude Code 相比直接使用 Cursor 或 GitHub Copilot，核心优势在哪里？为什么不直接用那些工具？
zh.answer: [Cursor](https://cursor.sh) 和 [GitHub Copilot](https://github.com/features/copilot) 本质上是「IDE 内的代码补全/对话工具」，上下文窗口受限（Cursor 约 100K，Copilot 更少），且均不支持真正的多智能体并发任务分解。Everything Claude Code 基于 Claude 200K+ Token 上下文窗口，通过多智能体架构实现真正意义上的「自主任务执行」——AI 可以同时调度研究员、架构师、安全审计员并行工作，而不是在单一对话中串行处理。此外，本项目的持久化记忆和生命周期钩子是 Cursor/Copilot 完全不具备的工程化能力。如果你的需求是行内代码补全，Copilot 足够；如果你需要 AI 参与整个软件工程生命周期，Everything Claude Code 是量级不同的解决方案。

zh.question: 多个智能体并发运行时，Token 消耗会爆炸式增长吗？有没有控制成本的方法？
zh.answer: 这是 GitHub Issues 和 Reddit 中讨论最热烈的话题之一。是的，多智能体并发确实会显著增加 Token 消耗，在不加控制的情况下，一次复杂任务可能消耗 50K-200K Token。推荐的 Workaround 有三个：1）在 CLAUDE.md 中明确定义「技能激活白名单」，只在需要时注入特定技能，避免全量技能注入；2）使用 Pre-Tool 钩子设置 Token 预算阈值，超出时强制中断并汇报进度；3）对于探索性任务使用 claude-haiku 模型进行 Agent 调度，只在最终代码生成阶段切换到 claude-sonnet，可节省 60%-70% 的 Token 成本。

zh.question: CLAUDE.md 文件应该写多少内容？是不是越详细越好？
zh.answer: 这是社区中存在明显争议的话题。过于详细的 CLAUDE.md 会占用宝贵的上下文窗口，反而可能稀释真正重要信息的权重。社区共识（来自 Reddit r/ClaudeCode）是：CLAUDE.md 应控制在 2000-3000 Token 以内，重点覆盖「架构决策」「禁止模式（Anti-Patterns）」「关键依赖版本」三类高价值信息；详细的技术文档应拆分至 /skills 目录以按需加载；避免把 README 直接复制进 CLAUDE.md。Everything Claude Code 在其 CLAUDE.md 模板中对此给出了详细的结构化规范，可直接参考使用。

zh.question: 安全红队扫描功能实际能检测哪些风险？它能替代专业的代码安全审计吗？
zh.answer: 内置的安全扫描技能主要针对三类 AI 编码特有的风险：1）提示注入（Prompt Injection）——检测代码中是否存在可被恶意用户通过输入劫持 AI 行为的模式；2）权限配置漂移——对比 MCP 配置中声明的最小权限与实际工具调用行为是否一致；3）AI 生成代码中的 OWASP Top10 漏洞模式识别，如硬编码密钥、SQL 拼接、不安全反序列化等。它不能替代专业的 SAST/DAST 工具（如 [Semgrep](https://semgrep.dev) 或 [Snyk](https://snyk.io)），而是作为「AI 原生风险」的第一道防线。建议将其作为 Pre-Edit 钩子融入 CI/CD 流程，与 Semgrep 形成互补。

zh.question: 为什么选择 JavaScript/TypeScript 作为主语言，而不是 Python？这对后端 Python 开发者有影响吗？
zh.answer: 项目框架层（钩子、命令、插件系统）使用 JavaScript/TypeScript 是因为 Claude Code CLI 本身基于 Node.js 生态，与 npm 插件市场深度集成。但这不影响 Python 开发者：所有针对 Python 项目的技能模块（如 FastAPI 架构技能、pytest 测试技能）均以纯 Markdown 编写，不依赖任何运行时；生命周期钩子可以调用任意 shell 命令，因此 Python lint（ruff/flake8）、测试（pytest）等工具均可无缝集成。实际上，Python 生态的技能和智能体在社区中是贡献最活跃的类别之一。

zh.question: 在团队协作场景中，如何解决不同开发者的 CLAUDE.md 和记忆文件冲突问题？
zh.answer: 这是项目当前被 GitHub Issues 讨论最多的架构痛点之一。现有的官方建议是将 CLAUDE.md 纳入 Git 版本控制（作为团队共享的「AI 规范文档」），而将 .claude/memory 目录加入 .gitignore（个人化的 AI 会话记忆不应共享）。对于需要团队级共享记忆的场景，社区发展出了一种「Memory Registry 模式」：在共享的 /contexts 目录下维护团队级知识库，通过 slash command 按需手动注入，而不是自动全量加载。这一方案的缺点是需要人工维护 contexts 目录的时效性，项目尚未提供自动化的团队记忆同步方案。

zh.question: Everything Claude Code 支持的 11 个命令里，哪几个对日常效率提升最显著？
zh.answer: 根据社区使用反馈，效率提升最显著的三个命令是：1）/research——触发 Research Agent 对给定技术问题进行深度互联网检索和代码库分析，输出结构化的「技术选型报告」，彻底取代手动查文档；2）/security-scan——一键触发安全红队扫描，在提交 PR 前发现 AI 引入的潜在漏洞；3）/architect——触发 Architect Agent 基于当前代码库状态和需求描述生成详细的「架构变更提案（ADR）」，使 AI 编码从「写代码」升级为「做工程决策」。这三个命令覆盖了「研究→决策→安全」的完整工程前置链路。

zh.question: 项目迭代很快，如何追踪哪些技能和智能体是最新推荐的，避免使用过时的配置？
zh.answer: 这是快速增长的开源项目面临的普遍挑战。实践建议：1）订阅仓库的 Release 通知，CHANGELOG.md 中对每个版本的技能/智能体变更有明确记录；2）通过 npm update -g everything-claude-code 保持最新版本，钩子系统在检测到版本更新时会自动提示；3）查阅 .claude-plugin 目录下的 manifest 文件，其中标注了每个组件的 stability 字段（stable/experimental/deprecated）；4）加入项目官方 Discord（链接见 README），核心维护者 Affaan Malik 会在重大配置变更时发布迁移指南。避免直接 fork 主分支在生产环境使用，优先通过插件市场安装以获得版本管理保障。

en.question: What is the core advantage of Everything Claude Code over using Cursor or GitHub Copilot directly? Why not just use those tools?
en.answer: [Cursor](https://cursor.sh) and [GitHub Copilot](https://github.com/features/copilot) are fundamentally 'code completion/chat tools within an IDE' with limited context windows (Cursor ~100K, Copilot even less) and no support for true multi-agent concurrent task decomposition. Everything Claude Code leverages Claude's 200K+ token context window and multi-agent architecture to enable genuinely autonomous task execution — AI can simultaneously dispatch a researcher, architect, and security auditor working in parallel, not serially in a single conversation. Furthermore, persistent memory and lifecycle hooks represent engineering capabilities entirely absent from Cursor/Copilot. If you need inline code completion, Copilot suffices; if you need AI involvement across the entire software engineering lifecycle, Everything Claude Code operates at a fundamentally different scale.

en.question: Will token consumption explode with multiple agents running concurrently? Are there cost-control strategies?
en.answer: This is one of the most hotly discussed topics in GitHub Issues and Reddit. Yes, multi-agent concurrency significantly increases token usage — an uncontrolled complex task can consume 50K-200K tokens. Three recommended workarounds: 1) Define a 'skill activation whitelist' in CLAUDE.md to inject only specific skills when needed, avoiding full-set injection; 2) Use Pre-Tool hooks to set token budget thresholds with forced interruption and progress reporting when exceeded; 3) Use claude-haiku for agent orchestration during exploratory tasks and switch to claude-sonnet only for final code generation, saving 60%-70% of token costs.

en.question: How much content should go in CLAUDE.md? Is more detail always better?
en.answer: This is a topic of clear community debate. An overly detailed CLAUDE.md consumes valuable context window space, potentially diluting the weight of truly important information. The community consensus (from Reddit r/ClaudeCode) is: keep CLAUDE.md under 2000-3000 tokens, focusing on three high-value categories: 'architectural decisions,' 'anti-patterns to avoid,' and 'critical dependency versions.' Detailed technical docs should be split into the /skills directory for on-demand loading. Never copy-paste a README directly into CLAUDE.md. Everything Claude Code provides detailed structural specifications in its CLAUDE.md template that can be used as a direct reference.

en.question: What risks does the security red-team scan actually detect? Can it replace professional code security audits?
en.answer: The built-in security scanning skill primarily targets three AI-coding-specific risk categories: 1) Prompt Injection — detecting patterns in code that malicious users could exploit to hijack AI behavior via inputs; 2) Permission config drift — comparing declared minimum permissions in MCP configs against actual tool-call behavior; 3) OWASP Top10 vulnerability pattern recognition in AI-generated code, such as hardcoded secrets, SQL concatenation, and insecure deserialization. It cannot replace professional SAST/DAST tools like [Semgrep](https://semgrep.dev) or [Snyk](https://snyk.io), but serves as the first line of defense against AI-native risks. The recommended approach is to integrate it as a Pre-Edit hook in CI/CD pipelines as a complement to Semgrep.

en.question: Why JavaScript/TypeScript as the primary language instead of Python? Does this affect Python backend developers?
en.answer: The framework layer (hooks, commands, plugin system) uses JavaScript/TypeScript because the Claude Code CLI itself is Node.js-based and deeply integrated with the npm plugin marketplace. However, this doesn't affect Python developers: all Python project skill modules (FastAPI architecture skills, pytest testing skills) are written in pure Markdown with no runtime dependencies; lifecycle hooks can invoke any shell command, so Python lint tools (ruff/flake8) and test runners (pytest) integrate seamlessly. In practice, Python ecosystem skills and agents are among the most actively contributed categories in the community.

en.question: In team collaboration scenarios, how do you resolve conflicts between different developers' CLAUDE.md files and memory files?
en.answer: This is one of the most discussed architectural pain points in GitHub Issues. The current official recommendation is to put CLAUDE.md under Git version control (as a shared team 'AI specification document') while adding .claude/memory to .gitignore (personalized AI session memory should not be shared). For team-level shared memory scenarios, the community has developed a 'Memory Registry Pattern': maintaining a team knowledge base under the shared /contexts directory, manually injected on demand via slash commands rather than auto-loaded in full. The downside is that it requires manual maintenance of the contexts directory's freshness — the project has not yet provided an automated team memory synchronization solution.

en.question: Among the 11 supported commands, which ones have the most significant impact on daily efficiency?
en.answer: Based on community usage feedback, the three commands with the most significant efficiency gains are: 1) /research — triggers the Research Agent to perform deep internet search and codebase analysis on a given technical problem, outputting a structured 'Technology Selection Report,' completely replacing manual documentation lookup; 2) /security-scan — one-click security red-team scan to identify AI-introduced vulnerabilities before submitting a PR; 3) /architect — triggers the Architect Agent to generate a detailed 'Architectural Decision Record (ADR)' based on current codebase state and requirements, elevating AI coding from 'writing code' to 'making engineering decisions.' These three commands cover the complete pre-engineering chain of research → decision → security.

en.question: The project iterates quickly — how do you track which skills and agents are the latest recommendations and avoid using outdated configurations?
en.answer: This is a common challenge for rapidly growing open-source projects. Practical advice: 1) Subscribe to repository Release notifications — CHANGELOG.md explicitly records skill/agent changes per version; 2) Run npm update -g everything-claude-code to stay current, as the hook system auto-prompts when a version update is detected; 3) Check manifest files in the .claude-plugin directory, which label each component's stability field (stable/experimental/deprecated); 4) Join the official Discord (linked in README), where core maintainer Affaan Malik publishes migration guides for major configuration changes. Avoid forking the main branch directly for production use — prefer installing via the plugin marketplace for version management guarantees.

seo_title_jsonb.en: Everything Claude Code: Ultimate 50K-Star Claude Code Enhancement Kit

seo_description_jsonb.zh: Anthropic 黑客马拉松冠军作品，9 智能体+11 技能+持久化记忆+安全红队，一行命令将 Claude Code 升级为企业级 AI 软件工程系统。
seo_description_jsonb.en: Anthropic hackathon winner's toolkit: 9 agents, 11 skills, persistent memory & security red-team scanning. One-line install to supercharge Claude Code for production-grade AI engineering.
